passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q. Nos. 40-50) Each of these question is followed by two statements numbered I and II. Decide whether the data given in the statements are sufficient to answer the question. Mark answer as
Give answer
(a) if Statement I alone is sufficient but Statement II alone is not sufficient to answer the question
(b) if Statement II alone is sufficient but Statement I alone is not sufficient to answer the question
(c) if both Statements I and II together are sufficient to answer the question but neither statement alone is sufficient
(d) if Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Six peoples S, T, U, V, W and X are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. What is T's position with respect to X?

choices.a: Only two people sit between U and W, X is second to the left of W, V and T are immediate neighbours of each other.
choices.b: T is to the immediate right of V. There are only two people between T and S. X is an immediate neighbour of S but not of V.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The ages of Aashima and Apsara are in the ratio 9 : 8. What is the age of Aashima?

choices.a: The ages of Aashima and Arpita are in the ratio 3 : 2.
choices.b: After 6 yr the ratio of Aashima's and Apsara's ages will be 11 : 10.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: D is the sister of C. How is D related to A?

choices.b: B is the brother of C.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the code for 'she' in the code language?

choices.a: In the code language 'she walks fast' is written as 'ka re pa'.
choices.b: In the code language 'do it fast' is written as 'jo ta re'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Tower 'A' is in which direction with respect to tower D?

choices.a: Tower B is to the South of tower A and to the East of tower C.
choices.b: Tower C is to the South of tower D.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How is 'light' coded in the code language?

choices.a: 'more light than dark' is coded as '3 2 1 7', 'no more dark here' is coded as '9 1 4 3'.
choices.b: 'no more than that' is coded as '3 0 4 7' and 'no than and that' is coded as '4 5 0 7'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many rooms does your house have?

choices.a: The number of rooms is the same as the number of family members in our house.
choices.b: The number is sufficient to accommodate our family members.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Who is the best salesman in the company?

choices.a: Rohit sold maximum number of air conditioners this summer.
choices.b: The company made the highest profit this year.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the colour of the curtains on the stage?

choices.a: The curtains have the same colour as the walls of the hall.
choices.b: The colour of the curtains is quite appealing.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Who is a better artist-Abid or Hussain?

choices.b: The number of paintings sold by Hussain is more.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Who types at a faster rate, Navin or Sapan?

choices.a: The difference between their typing rates is 10 words per minute
choices.b: Sapan types at a constant rate of 80 words per minute.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If we assume a constant reading rate, can Joel finish the book in 6 h?

choices.b: In 2 h, he reads half the book.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a certain packing houses, grapefruit are packed in bags and the bags are packed in cases. How many grapefruits are in each case, that is packed?

choices.a: The grapefruits always packed 5 to a bag and the bags are always packed 8 to a case.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How many hits must a batsman get to raise his batting average to 300?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: For a certain bottle and cork, what is the price of the cork?

choices.a: The combined price of the bottle and the cork is ₹ 95.
choices.b: The price of the bottle is ₹ 75 more than the price of the cork.
